outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: KC-135 Crash Marks 4th US Aircraft Lost — Black Rain Falls Across Iran and Iraq
outlets.summary: Leads with KC-135 tanker crash — 4th US aircraft lost in the conflict. Reports on 'black rain' environmental crisis from burning oil infrastructure. Frames Day 13 around mounting US military losses and humanitarian toll.

outlets.name: PBS
outlets.headline: France Mourns First Soldier Killed in Iraq as Iran Launches Most Intense Missile Operation
outlets.summary: Covers French soldier death in Iraq as first non-US NATO casualty. Reports Iran's most intense missile operation of the war. Analysis examines whether NATO could be drawn further into the conflict.

outlets.name: NBC News
outlets.headline: Brent Crude Holds Above $100 as IEA's Record 400M Barrel Release Fails to Contain Oil Surge
outlets.summary: Markets and energy focus — Brent above $100 despite IEA's largest-ever 400M barrel release across 32 countries. US contributing 172M from SPR. Two more tankers hit in Iraqi waters. 280+ bulk carriers stranded.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump: War Will End 'Soon,' Oil Disruption Would Trigger 'Harsher Strikes' Against Iran
outlets.summary: Leads with Trump's confidence the war is ending soon. Frames oil disruption warning as show of strength. Covers KC-135 crash but emphasizes continued US military dominance.

outlets.name: Daily Wire
outlets.headline: Trump Says 'Practically Nothing Left to Target' — Iran's Peace Conditions DOA
outlets.summary: Pro-operation framing — Trump's 'nothing left to target' claim. Dismisses Pezeshkian's 3 peace conditions as non-starters. Covers oil at $100 but argues military victory will restore markets.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Black Rain, Burning Refineries, and a $100 Barrel: Day 13 of the Iran War
outlets.summary: Environmental and economic focus — black rain from burning petroleum infrastructure across Iran, Iraq, and Kuwait. Brent past $100. IEA 400M barrel release struggles to offset Hormuz blockade.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran's President Sets Terms to End the War — Is an Off-Ramp in Sight?
outlets.summary: Covers Pezeshkian's 3 peace conditions: recognition, guarantees, reparations. Frames as first concrete off-ramp but notes Araghchi's rejection of ceasefire contradicts the proposal. French soldier death and NATO expansion fears.

outlets.name: France 24
outlets.headline: French Soldier Killed in Iraq Drone Attack — Macron Calls Death 'Unacceptable'
outlets.summary: Leads with French angle — soldier killed in Iraqi militia drone attack on counterterrorism base. Macron demands accountability. Also covers KC-135 crash, Pezeshkian's peace conditions, and Iran's most intense missile operation.

outlets.name: CNBC
outlets.headline: Oil Prices Jump as IEA Record Reserve Release Fails to Calm Markets Amid Hormuz Blockade
outlets.summary: Markets focus — Brent at $100.46, WTI at $95.73. IEA's 400M barrel release across 32 countries deemed insufficient. US contributing 172M from SPR. Two more tankers hit in Iraqi waters. Analysts doubt reserves can offset Hormuz closure.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Trump: Iran War Could Be Over Soon, But Oil Disruption Would Trigger Harsher US Strikes
outlets.summary: Covers Trump's claim war ending soon contrasted with Israeli Defence Minister Katz's 'no time limit.' Reports on multi-layered missile defense intercepting Iran's most intense operation. France's first casualty raises NATO expansion concerns.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: IDF Intercepts Iran's Most Intense Missile Barrage — Arrow and David's Sling Systems Tested
outlets.summary: Defense focus — reports on IDF intercepting Iran's most intense missile operation to date. Analysis of Arrow-3 and David's Sling performance. Covers Pezeshkian's peace terms but Israeli officials dismiss them as non-credible.

outlets.name: Ynet News
outlets.headline: Home Front Update: Sirens Continue as Iran Tests Limits of Israel's Air Defense
outlets.summary: Home-front focus — siren frequency and interceptor success rates. Reports on public shelter fatigue entering Day 13. Finance Ministry warns of economic impact as Brent crosses $100.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Iran Launches Most Intense Missile Operation — Resistance Forces Strike Across Region
outlets.summary: Frames Iran's most intense missile operation as proof of unbroken military capability. Claims widespread damage to Israeli and US targets. Emphasizes Pezeshkian's peace conditions as 'reasonable' while Western media ignores them.

outlets.name: Tehran Times
outlets.headline: President Pezeshkian Offers Peace — US-Israel Regime Responds With More Bombs
outlets.summary: Frames Pezeshkian's 3 peace conditions as magnanimous offer rejected by aggressors. Highlights KC-135 crash as evidence of US military weakness. Claims black rain is result of US-Israeli targeting of civilian oil infrastructure.

outlets.name: IRNA
outlets.headline: Islamic Republic Demonstrates Full Military Capability on Day 13 of Aggression
outlets.summary: State agency frames Iran's missile operation as demonstrating unbroken capability despite 13 days of strikes. Emphasizes the French soldier death as proof the war is expanding beyond US-Israel. Covers oil at $100 as economic pressure on aggressors.
